prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a nearly blank, dark green frame. A “Classic Commercial Archives” watermark is visible at the lower left, and a faint archive watermark runs across the image.

After this, the scene cuts to a small bathroom or washroom setting. The space has light-colored wallpaper above blue wall tiles. A white shelf or ledge holds a wicker basket, and there is a small plant near a sink area. On the right side of the frame, a hand holds a dark brown bottle, angled toward the bathroom fixtures. A framed picture hangs on the wall.

The next shot moves closer to a sink or vanity area. A medicine cabinet or mirrored cabinet appears open above the sink, with shelves and objects inside. A wicker basket remains visible on the left. A hand on the right holds the same dark brown bottle over or near the sink counter, suggesting the bottle is being used in the bathroom area, though the exact action is not fully clear from the sampled frame.

The following frame shows another bathroom-related image, partly overlaid or in transition. A white toilet is visible on the right, with the dark brown bottle angled near it. On the lower left, there appear to be product packages or containers grouped together, but the image is soft and partially obscured by the dissolve effect.

The commercial then shifts to a graphic/product display. Large black text at the top reads: “Where is your brown bottle of Lysol?” Below the text, an inset image shows a woman indoors, facing the camera. To the right of the inset, a large dark brown Lysol bottle is displayed beside a tall rectangular package or box. The frame has a layered, slightly faded look, as if transitioning into the final product shot.

The final frame presents the end layout more clearly: the same headline, “Where is your brown bottle of Lysol?”, appears across the top. On the left is a rectangular inset of a woman speaking or posing in an indoor home setting. On the right is a prominent brown Lysol bottle with its label visible, placed next to a tall product box. The archive watermark remains visible over the image.
